# Operator interpretation of resonances generated by some operator matrices

> We consider the analytic continuation of the transfer function for a 2x2 matrix Hamiltonian into the unphysical sheets of the energy Riemann surface. We construct a family of non-selfadjoint operators which reproduce certain parts of the transfer-function spectrum including resonances situated on the unphysical sheets neighboring the physical sheet. On this basis, completeness and basis properties for the root vectors of the transfer function (including those for the resonances) are proved.

## Key claim

The paper proves that for a 2x2 operator matrix Hamiltonian where the spectra of the diagonal entries overlap (specifically, where the spectrum of one entry is partly or totally embedded in the continuous spectrum of the other), one can construct up to 2^m non-selfadjoint operators whose spectra include the resonances of the transfer function on the unphysical sheets neighboring the physical sheet, provided the coupling operators satisfy certain analytic continuation and boundedness conditions.

## Method

The authors consider a 2x2 self-adjoint operator matrix H acting on a direct sum of Hilbert spaces, with diagonal entries A_0 and A_1 and bounded off-diagonal couplings B_01 and B_10. They analytically continue the transfer function M_1(z) through the spectral intervals of A_0 into the unphysical sheets of the Riemann surface, assuming the derivative of the spectral measure of A_0 with respect to the couplings admits analytic continuation. They then solve the nonlinear operator equation Y = A_1 + V_1(Y, Gamma) for each of the 2^m possible choices of contours Gamma_l in the unphysical sheets, where m is the number of spectral intervals of A_0, and prove that the solutions H_1^(l) = A_1 + X^(l) are non-selfadjoint operators whose spectra coincide with the spectrum of the continued transfer function in a neighborhood of sigma(A_1).

## Key numbers

* The solvability condition for the basic equation is: V_0(B, Gamma) < (1/4) d_0^2(Gamma), where d_0(Gamma) = dist{sigma(A_1), sigma'(A_0) union Gamma} and V_0(B, Gamma) is the modified variation of the coupling operators with respect to the spectral measure of A_0.
* The solution X of the basic equation satisfies ||X|| <= r_min(Gamma), where r_min(Gamma) = d_0(Gamma)/2 - sqrt(d_0^2(Gamma)/4 - V_0(B, Gamma)).
* The radius r_0(B) = inf_{Gamma_l: omega(B,Gamma_l)>0} r_min(Gamma_l) with omega(B,Gamma_l) = d_0^2(Gamma_l) - 4 V_0(B,Gamma_l) does not depend on the multi-index l.
* The norm of the operator Omega^(l) satisfies ||Omega^(l)|| < V_0(B,Gamma_l) / (d_0(Gamma_l)/2)^2 < 1.
* The spectrum of H_1^(l) lies in the closed r_0(B)-vicinity of sigma(A_1), i.e., {z in C: dist{z, sigma(A_1)} <= r_0(B)}.

## Context

This work extends the earlier results of Motovilov (1995, 1996) and Mennicken and Motovilov (1996) on the operator interpretation of resonances, which were restricted to the case where the spectra of A_0 and A_1 are separated by a positive distance. It also generalizes the block-diagonalization approach of Okubo (1954), Malyshev and Minlos (1985), and Adamyan and Langer (1995) to the overlapping-spectrum case. The paper enables the study of completeness and basis properties of resonance eigenvectors using the spectral theory of non-selfadjoint operators, building on the factorization theorem of Virozub and Matsaev (1977).
